component.name: Horizon
component.summary: Multi-provider AI chat — Ollama, Claude, OpenAI, Gemini

description.p: Horizon is a Flutter chat client that talks to Ollama, Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini from a single app. Pick provider and model per conversation, switch mid-thread, and keep API keys in the OS keystore.

ul.li: Four providers in one client: Ollama (local), Claude (Anthropic), OpenAI, Gemini (Google)
Per-chat provider, model, system prompt, and inference options
Primary + backup Ollama URL with automatic failover — keep your home-LAN server private and fall back to a VPN/Tailscale endpoint transparently
API keys stored in the OS keystore via libsecret
Smooth streaming output with typewriter buffering
Edit and regenerate any message in the conversation

description.p: Horizon is a fork of Reins by Ibrahim Çetin. The original Ollama-focused client and Flutter scaffolding are his work; Horizon extends it into a multi-provider client with hardened networking and self-hosted-friendly features.

ul.li: First Linux release: .deb for Debian/Ubuntu/Mint/Pop and .tar.gz for other distros.
Fixed a blank-screen flash between sending a prompt and the first streamed token.
Fixed sticky touch scrolling on 120 Hz screens during long streams.
Restored platform-native overscroll feel (Android stretch, iOS bounce).
Stopped forcing num_ctx=2048 on every Ollama request, which was making the server unload and reload models that were already running at a different context size.

description.p: Scroll engine rewrite, Gemini provider, backup Ollama URL.

ul.li: Rewrote the chat scroll engine — long responses no longer feel like a slideshow during or after generation.
Added Gemini (Google Generative Language API) as a fourth provider.
Added an optional backup Ollama server URL with automatic failover, so a private home-LAN address can stay private while traffic transparently routes via VPN/Tailscale when out-of-network.
Windows builds now bundle the VC++ runtime DLLs so horizon.exe launches without a separate Redistributable install.
